My Biodata

Name Reyner
Age 15 Years Old
Address Indonesia, Bandung, THI
Hobby Badminton, Futsal, and gym
School Bintang Mulia
Class 9D

Bandung is the best city in Indonesia.
Nestled among cool highland hills, Bandung wakes each morning beneath a soft veil of mist. The air feels different here—lighter, touched by mountain breezes drifting down from Tangkuban Perahu, the legendary volcano said to have been born from a broken heart. From its crater, steam rises like whispers of old Sundanese tales, reminding the city of its ancient roots. As the sun climbs higher, the streets begin to hum. Along Jalan Braga, colonial-era buildings stand proudly, their art deco facades painted in warm hues. Artists set up easels on the sidewalks, capturing the golden light that dances across vintage windows. Cafés open their doors, releasing the rich aroma of coffee brewed from beans grown in nearby hills. Laughter spills onto the pavement as friends gather, sharing stories over plates of steaming batagor. By afternoon, the city reveals its modern pulse. Students from Institut Teknologi Bandung hurry past, carrying dreams as vast as the sky above Gedung Sate’s towering spire. Creativity thrives here; fashion boutiques and factory outlets line the avenues, earning Bandung its playful nickname, Paris van Java. Colors burst from every corner—bright hijabs, painted murals, neon café signs—blending tradition with youthful ambition. As evening falls, the temperature drops and the city glows. Families stroll through leafy parks, and street musicians strum gentle melodies beneath flickering lamps. From hilltop viewpoints, Bandung stretches out like a constellation, lights shimmering against the dark silhouette of mountains. In that quiet moment, the city feels both intimate and infinite—a place where history, nature, and imagination meet. And as night settles in, Bandung dreams, cradled by the highlands that have always called it home.

Bandung is the heaven of food culinary

One cool afternoon in Bandung, a traveler wandered through a lively food street, drawn by the irresistible aroma of Sundanese cooking. The journey began with a traditional meal of Nasi Timbel. Warm rice wrapped in fragrant banana leaves was served with vegetables, sambal, and savory side dishes. Each bite tasted like home, rich with the flavors of West Java. Next came a crispy snack loved by locals: Cireng. The golden, chewy fritters were dipped into a spicy sauce that balanced sweetness and heat. Simple yet addictive, they showed how Bandung’s street food could turn humble ingredients into something special. Nearby, a vendor prepared steaming bowls of Seblak. The broth bubbled with chili, garlic, noodles, and crackers softened in the spicy soup. Its bold aroma filled the air, warming everyone in the cool mountain climate. Not far away, a famous Bandung specialty appeared on bright orange plates: Batagor. Crispy tofu and fish dumplings were smothered in thick peanut sauce and sprinkled with lime juice. The combination was rich, nutty, and unforgettable. Finally, the meal ended with a hearty serving of Ayam Goreng, crispy on the outside and tender inside. As evening lights glowed across Bandung, the traveler realized the city’s true magic lived not only in its mountains—but also in its food. 🍛✨

Most popular drinks in Bandung

On a sunny afternoon in Bandung, two friends decided to explore the city’s famous drinks. Their journey started with a glass of Es Susu Stroberi. The drink was pink, sweet, and full of ice, making it the perfect refreshment in the warm afternoon. Next, they visited a traditional stall and ordered Bajigur. The warm mixture of coconut milk, palm sugar, and ginger gave a comforting taste. Even though Bandung is known for its cool weather, the warm drink felt relaxing and reminded them of Sundanese traditions. Later, they stopped at a modern café and tried a colorful Green Tea Latte topped with creamy foam. The drink looked beautiful and tasted both sweet and slightly bitter, making it popular among young people in the city. After walking around the busy streets, they refreshed themselves with a tall glass of Es Teh Manis with a slice of orange. The cold tea was simple but very refreshing. Finally, they ended their drink adventure with a bowl of Es Campur. Inside were colorful toppings like jelly, fruit, and sweet syrup mixed with shaved ice. As the sun began to set behind the mountains surrounding Bandung, the friends realized that the city’s drinks were not just refreshing—they were part of Bandung’s culture and joyful lifestyle. 🍹

Historical places in Bandung

One morning in Bandung, a young traveler began exploring the city’s famous landmarks. His first stop was Gedung Merdeka, a historic place where important world leaders once gathered during the Asian–African Conference. Standing in front of the building, he imagined the moment when nations came together to talk about peace and cooperation. After that, he walked to the iconic Gedung Sate. The building’s unique tower looked beautiful under the bright blue sky. Locals often take photos there because it is one of the main symbols of Bandung. In the afternoon, he visited the peaceful Bandung Grand Mosque. The tall twin towers stood proudly beside the large green square. Families and visitors relaxed in the open field while enjoying the fresh air. Not far away, he continued to the Monumen Perjuangan Rakyat Jawa Barat, a monument that honors the bravery of the people who fought for Indonesia’s independence. Before the day ended, he also saw the tall Monumen Bandung Lautan Api, which remembers the historic Bandung Sea of Fire. At night, he walked along the lively street of Jalan Braga. The old colonial buildings glowed under the street lights, creating a romantic atmosphere. By the end of the day, he realized that Bandung was not just a beautiful city—it was a place full of history, culture, and unforgettable stories. ✨

University in Bandung

One bright morning in Bandung, a high school student set out to explore the city's prestigious academic landscape, starting at the iconic Universitas Padjadjaran (UNPAD) on Jalan Dipati Ukur, where the stately main building inspired dreams of a future in law or social sciences. The journey continued to Universitas Pasundan (UNPAS), where the tall, modern facade and its signature red accents bustled with the energy of students rushing to class. Nearby, the sleek glass architecture of Institut Teknologi Bandung (ITB) shimmered under the sun, symbolizing the city's reputation as a hub for innovation and engineering excellence. The student then visited Universitas Islam Bandung (UNISBA), where scholars in light blue blazers stood in front of the blue-trimmed campus, engaged in deep discussions that showcased the lively academic spirit of the community. A short trip north led to the breathtaking Villa Isola at Universitas Pendidikan Indonesia (UPI), an Art Deco masterpiece that looked like a white palace against the bright blue sky. Standing there, looking out over the sprawling green campus nestled among the lush hills of Lembang, the student realized that Bandung is truly a city where historic beauty and a passion for learning come together to inspire the next generation. 🎓✨

Museums in Bandung

One sunny weekend in Bandung, a curious traveler set out on a mission to explore the city’s rich history through its most iconic museums and landmarks. The journey was more than just a walk through the streets; it was a trip back in time to discover the stories that shaped Indonesia.

Culinary spots

As the sun began to set over the city, the student’s appetite for adventure led them toward the tantalizing aromas and neon lights of Bandung’s famous food scene. This journey wasn’t just about eating; it was a deep dive into the flavors that make this city a culinary capital. By the end of the trip, the student realized that in Bandung, food is more than just a meal—it’s a way of connecting with the city’s history, its people, and its stunning landscapes. 🍜⛰️✨

Natural parks

A Journey Through Mist and Flavors The day started early at Tebing Keraton, where the student stood on a rocky cliff overlooking a vast sea of pine trees. The sunrise painted the sky in shades of gold and purple, while the fog drifted through the valley of Tahura, creating a scene that felt like a dream. It was a moment of pure silence, broken only by the chirping of birds and the rustle of the morning breeze. Driven by a sense of adventure, the traveler headed further north to the majestic Tangkuban Perahu. Standing at the edge of the massive Queen’s Crater (Kawah Ratu), the student marveled at the swirling white smoke and the distinct scent of sulfur. The legendary mountain, resembling an overturned boat, stood as a powerful reminder of nature’s ancient strength and the folk tales that local people still hold dear. The exploration continued south to the ethereal Kawah Putih in Ciwidey. The turquoise lake, framed by white sand and skeletal trees, looked like a landscape from another planet. The student walked along the wooden bridge, feeling the cool mountain air and capturing the surreal beauty of the volcanic crater, which has fascinated travelers for generations.

My first paragraph.
